 {"id":"git_safety_guard-5apz.2","title":"[Suggest] Audit logging for suggested allowlist entries","description":"# Task: Audit logging for suggested allowlist entries

## Purpose
Ensure every accepted suggestion is recorded for traceability and review.

## Requirements
- Log accepted suggestion to history with: rule_id (if any), pattern, risk score,
  user action (accepted/modified), and timestamp
- Include source cluster stats (frequency, sample commands)

## Implementation
- Extend history schema for suggestion events or reuse existing logging channel
- Update suggest-allowlist CLI to emit audit entries on accept

## Acceptance Criteria
- Accepting a suggestion writes an audit record
- Non-interactive mode produces **no writes**

Accepting a suggestion now logs SuggestionAuditEntry with pattern, risk score, confidence, cluster stats to history database. Skipping logs as Rejected. Non-interactive mode produces no writes.  {"id":"git_safety_guard-5apz.3","title":"[Suggest] Unit tests for clustering + pattern + risk scoring","description":"# Task: Unit tests for clustering + pattern + risk scoring

## Purpose
Ensure core suggestion algorithms behave deterministically and safely.

## Requirements
- Unit tests for clustering (grouping correctness)
- Unit tests for pattern generation (anchoring, no overbroad matches)
- Unit tests for risk scoring rules

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests include negative cases (unsafe commands not generalized)
- Output is deterministic across runs

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-5apz.4","title":"[Suggest] E2E tests for suggest-allowlist CLI + verbose logs","description":"# Task: E2E tests for suggest-allowlist CLI + verbose logs

## Purpose
Validate end-to-end behavior of suggest-allowlist, including interactive and
non-interactive output paths, with detailed logs on failure.

## Requirements
- Fixture history DB or log with known denied commands
- Tests for JSON output (non-interactive)
- Tests for interactive flow (accept/skip) using scripted input
- Ensure no allowlist writes occur in non-interactive mode

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E tests pass with clear, verbose failure logs
- CLI behavior matches documented contract

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-5f5","title":"[PACK] secrets.doppler - Doppler CLI Pack","description":"# Doppler CLI Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Doppler secrets management CLI.

## Destructive Patterns
- `doppler secrets delete` - Deletes secrets
- `doppler projects delete` - Deletes projects
- `doppler environments delete` - Deletes environments
- `doppler configs delete` - Deletes configs

## Safe Patterns
- `doppler secrets` (list), `doppler secrets get`
- `doppler run` - Injects secrets
- `doppler projects`, `doppler environments`
- `doppler configure`, `doppler setup`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: secrets.* (git_safety_guard-a8v)
- Keywords: doppler

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All delete operations blocked
- [ ] Secret injection allowed
- [ ] Tests cover common Doppler workflows","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:31:04.644344211-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T17:38:54.393214581-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T17:38:54.393214581-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: added secrets.doppler pack + tests and registered in pack registry. Docs update delegated to mbq7.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-5f5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:34.301355834-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-5f5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:34.34167744-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-5f5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:34.383142858-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-5f5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:34.425845303-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-5f5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:34.466111014-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-5f5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:34.505493332-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-5f5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:09.856433543-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-5f5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:09.902136802-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-5hz9","title":"Improve span classification to recognize string literal contexts","description":"## Objective

Enhance the context/span classification system to properly identify and handle string literal contexts where dangerous patterns should not trigger denial.

## Context Types to Handle

1. Single-quoted strings: 'dangerous text here'
2. Double-quoted strings: "dangerous text here"  
3. Dollar-quoted strings: $'...'
4. Heredocs: <<EOF ... EOF
5. Command arguments that are clearly data, not code

## Implementation Approach

1. Review classify_command in src/context.rs
2. Add new span kinds for string literal content
3. Ensure pattern matching only runs against executable spans
4. Add tests for each context type

## Acceptance Criteria

- String literals correctly classified as non-executable
- Pattern matching respects span classification
- No regression in detecting actual dangerous commands
- Tests cover all string context types","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:14:58.707446109-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T00:39:21.703171383-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T00:39:21.703171383-05:00","close_reason":"Completed as part of heredoc false positive fix","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-5hz9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1x7y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:15:51.268400593-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-5ib","title":"Write architecture decision document (ADR)","description":"# Write Architecture Decision Document (ADR)

## Objective

Synthesize research findings into a formal Architecture Decision Record that guides implementation.

## Background

After completing research on ast-grep, tree-sitter, and design tasks, we need to document the chosen approach and rationale. This ADR will be the reference for all implementation work.

## ADR Structure

### 1. Title

"ADR-001: Heredoc Scanning Architecture"

### 2. Status

Proposed → Accepted → Implemented

### 3. Context

- Problem statement (heredoc/inline-script bypasses)
- Constraints (performance, dependencies, maintenance, false positives)
- Options considered

### 4. Decision

- Integration approach (embedded vs external)
- Pattern storage format
- Integration points with existing dcg architecture

### 5. Consequences

- Benefits of chosen approach
- Drawbacks and mitigations
- Future considerations

### 6. Technical Details

- Dependency changes to Cargo.toml
- New modules and their responsibilities
- Data flow through the system
- Configuration schema additions

## Key Decisions to Document

### Integration Approach

- CLI invocation vs library
- Sync vs async processing
- Caching strategy

### Language Support

- Initial language set
- Grammar distribution
- Extension mechanism

### Pattern Format

- Hardcoded vs external
- File format (YAML, TOML, custom)
- Validation approach

### Performance Strategy

- Quick-reject optimizations
- Lazy loading
- Timeout handling

### Error Handling

- Parse failures
- Unknown languages
- Pattern match errors

## Deliverables

- ADR document (location TBD by repo conventions)
- Diagram showing data flow
- Short section that explains how this interacts with:
  - execution-context classification
  - severity taxonomy / decision modes
  - limits/timeouts/fail-open policy

## Acceptance Criteria

- ADR is self-contained and specific enough that implementation can proceed without re-reading external plan docs.
- ADR makes an explicit choice for:
  - embedded vs external AST engine
  - language set + extension model
  - pattern format + validation
- ADR includes:
  - performance budget and how it’s enforced
  - false-positive mitigation strategy
  - failure-mode behavior (timeout/parse error) especially in hook mode
","notes":"ADR complete. See docs/adr-001-heredoc-scanning.md. Covers: tiered architecture (Tier 0-3), ast-grep-core integration, pattern library design, performance budgets, fail-open semantics, and integration points.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:30:31.162229073-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T00:31:21.134276063-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T00:31:21.134276063-05:00","close_reason":"ADR-001 complete at docs/adr-001-heredoc-scanning.md","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-5ib","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-b45","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:30:37.437715869-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-5ib","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-2j3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:30:37.457081439-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-5ib","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-o15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:30:37.477144052-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-5ib","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jfj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:30:37.496865872-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-5ib","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6sg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:30:37.516274063-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-5ib","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-boy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:39:09.196463586-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-5ib","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-yza","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:49:43.694285861-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
